title: Energy approach to Coulomb gases and log gases
speaker: Sylvia Serfaty

proof_stages.label: Formal sine-Gordon measure and stochastic quantization
proof_stages.summary: The talk introduces the two-dimensional sine-Gordon Gibbs measure as a perturbation of the Gaussian free field and motivates the associated Langevin-type SPDE.

proof_stages.label: Known phase diagram and target regime
proof_stages.summary: Known results are organized around the thresholds β²=4π and β²=8π, motivating the goal of constructing the dynamics beyond the classical β²<4π regime.

proof_stages.label: Renormalized approximation and main convergence theorem
proof_stages.summary: The regularized equation is rescaled by the weak-renormalization factor ε^{-β²/(4π)}, and the main theorem asserts universality and convergence, globally for β²<4π and locally up to β²<16π/3.

proof_stages.label: Free-field decomposition and rough coefficient equation
proof_stages.summary: Writing U=Φ+v absorbs the singular scaling into Ψ_ε=ε^{-β²/(4π)}e^{iβΦ_ε} and reduces the problem to a PDE for v driven by random distributional coefficients.

proof_stages.label: Regularity computation and critical thresholds
proof_stages.summary: Negative Hölder spaces and Schauder/product estimates show that classical PDE theory requires η<1, while a Gaussian covariance calculation predicts Ψ_ε has regularity just below -β²/(4π), explaining the 4π and 8π thresholds.

proof_stages.label: Higher-moment convergence of the driving field
proof_stages.summary: The second-moment heuristic is upgraded to convergence of Ψ_ε in C^{-η} for all η>β²/(4π), requiring uniform control of all higher moments and screening-type cancellations.

proof_stages.label: Renormalized ill-posed equation and sine-cosine cancellation
proof_stages.summary: Above β²=4π unrenormalized rough products fail, but a general counterterm produces convergence, and for the sine-Gordon nonlinearity the counterterm cancels by the sine/cosine structure.

proof_stages.label: Abstract factorization and explicit renormalized objects
proof_stages.summary: A general solution-map factorization separates an explicit discontinuous model-building map from a continuous PDE map, reducing the proof to convergence of finitely many renormalized multilinear objects such as heat-kernel convolutions of Ψ_ε products.

examples.name: Neutral Coulomb gas interpretation
examples.note: The neutral gas of positive and negative charges illustrates why the sine-Gordon partition function has temperature regimes described by plasma behavior and dipole collapse, but the proof is formulated in SPDE terms.
examples.engagement_reason: the neutral Coulomb gas is used as a physical interpretation of the partition function and phase intuition, without doing a concrete calculation on the gas itself

passes.reason: The neutral Coulomb gas is used as a standard physical interpretation of the sine-Gordon partition function, not to refute a claim.

examples.engagement_reason: the finite-dimensional Langevin equation is cited as an analogy for deriving the SPDE, with only the standard invariant-measure fact stated

examples.name: General periodic forcing instead of sine
examples.note: Replacing sine by a finite trigonometric polynomial illustrates the robustness and universality of the limiting SPDE under perturbations of the microscopic periodic potential.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker applies the convergence discussion to a general periodic forcing, reasoning through Fourier harmonics, which modes survive or vanish, and when extra parameters would appear

passes.reason: Replacing sine by a general periodic finite trigonometric forcing is presented as a robustness/universality extension, not as a pathological case.

examples.name: Unrenormalized linear multiplicative equation
examples.note: The comparison equation d_tv=(1/2)Δv+vΨ^c_ε fails to converge above 4π, thereby revealing the need for renormalizing counterterms and the special sine-cosine cancellation.
examples.engagement_reason: the unrenormalized linear multiplicative equation is introduced as a contrast and its nonconvergence is stated, but no calculation or construction is carried out on it

passes.reason: The unrenormalized linear multiplicative equation is explicitly used to show that convergence fails without the special cancellation or correction terms.
